page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server allocates 2 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The CPU features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves average performance in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside average single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core execution is relatively weak, as shown by lower scores in multi-core Geekbench, stress-ng, and software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth also registers in the lower tier for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations, though the Passmark memory score remains in the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the c3-highmem-4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. Tradeoffs include limited multi-threaded scaling and lower database operation throughput, as evidenced by weak Passmark database and Redis SET benchmark results. This configuration is best suited for memory-intensive applications with moderate compute requirements, such as caching layers, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading and a 105 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU and text processing performance, contrasted with weaker multi-core execution, software compilation, and memory bandwidth. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, the c3-highmem-4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not demand heavy parallel compute. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications with moderate processing needs, such as caching layers, static web serving, and single-threaded data processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for memory-bound workloads.

Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support

No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
